title_en: Ibn al-Qayyim and the Book of Divine Wisdom
title_id: Ibn al-Qayyim dan Kitab Hikmah Ilahi
content_id:
Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah (691–751 H / 1292–1350 M) adalah salah satu ulama paling produktif dan orisinal dalam sejarah pemikiran Islam. Seorang murid dan teman dekat Ibn Taimiyyah selama hampir dua dekade, ia menyerap kerangka teologi Athari gurunya dan kritiknya terhadap filsafat kalam, sambil mengembangkan suaranya sendiri yang khas dalam etika, spiritualitas, dan hubungan antara posisi teologis dan pengalaman religius yang dihayati.

Shifa al-Alil fi Masa'il al-Qada wal-Qadar wat-Ta'lil wal-Hikma wal-Ta'lil — Menyembuhkan yang Sakit dalam Persoalan Takdir Ilahi, Qadar, Sebab-Akibat, dan Hikmah — adalah pembahasan komprehensif Ibn al-Qayyim tentang hikmah dan takdir Ilahi. Metafora medis dalam judul tersebut — menyembuhkan yang sakit — menunjukkan orientasi praktis karya ini: pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang dibahasnya bukan sekadar akademis melainkan menyentuh kebutuhan manusia yang paling dalam akan makna di tengah penderitaan, ketidakadilan, dan ketersembunyian Ilahi.

Pertanyaan-pertanyaan inti dalam Shifa al-Alil berkaitan dengan hubungan antara hikmah Ilahi, kehendak Ilahi (iradah), dan takdir Ilahi (qadar). Apakah Allah memiliki tujuan dalam segala sesuatu yang Dia tetapkan? Apakah perbuatan-Nya memiliki hikmah dan kebaikan sebagai tujuannya, ataukah kehendak Allah adalah kedaulatan mutlak tanpa hubungan dengan tujuan apa pun di luar diri-Nya sendiri? Bagaimana seorang mukmin seharusnya memahami penderitaan, kejahatan, dan ketidakadilan yang tampak di dunia ini dalam terang keyakinan akan Allah yang Maha Mengetahui, Mahakuasa, dan Mahabijaksana?

Pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini berada di pusat perdebatan teologis besar dalam sejarah Islam. Kaum Mu'tazilah berpendapat bahwa Allah berkewajiban atas keadilan untuk melakukan yang terbaik bagi makhluk-Nya dan tidak dapat menetapkan ketidakadilan. Mazhab Asy'ari menolak kewajiban apa pun atas Allah tetapi menegaskan bahwa perbuatan Allah terhubung dengan hikmah dan tujuan meskipun tujuan-tujuan tersebut tidak secara rasional bersifat mengikat. Posisi-posisi ekstrem tertentu dalam kalangan tradisionalis maupun filosofis memunculkan bayangan tentang takdir Ilahi yang sepenuhnya terputus dari tujuan yang dapat dipahami — kehendak kedaulatan murni yang melampaui semua kategori hikmah atau keadilan.

Pendekatan Ibn al-Qayyim menempuh jalan melalui perdebatan-perdebatan ini yang khas dalam penekanannya pada hikmah Ilahi sebagai kualitas yang positif dan dapat diketahui — bukan sebatas peniadaan antropomorfisme melainkan atribut Allah yang nyata yang memberikan pencerahan sejati tentang mengapa segala sesuatu sebagaimana adanya.
content_en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah (691–751 AH / 1292–1350 CE) was one of the most prolific and original scholars in the history of Islamic thought. A student and close companion of Ibn Taymiyyah for nearly two decades, he absorbed his teacher's Athari theological framework and his critique of kalam philosophy while developing his own distinctive voice in ethics, spirituality, and the relationship between theological positions and lived religious experience.

Shifa al-Alil fi Masa'il al-Qada wal-Qadar wat-Ta'lil wal-Hikma wal-Ta'lil — Healing the Sick in Questions of Divine Decree, Predetermination, Causation, and Wisdom — is Ibn al-Qayyim's comprehensive treatment of divine wisdom and decree. The title's medical metaphor — healing the sick — suggests the work's practical orientation: the questions it addresses are not merely academic but speak to some of the deepest human needs for meaning in the face of suffering, injustice, and divine hiddenness.

The core questions of Shifa al-Alil concern the relationship between divine wisdom (hikmah), divine will (iradah), and divine decree (qadar). Does God have a purpose in everything He decrees? Do His acts have wisdom and goodness as their ends, or is God's will an absolute sovereignty without any relationship to purposes beyond the divine self? How should a believer understand suffering, evil, and the apparent injustices of this world in light of belief in an all-knowing, all-powerful, and all-wise God?

These questions had been at the center of the great theological controversies of Islamic history. The Mu'tazila held that God is obligated by justice to do what is best for His creatures and cannot decree injustice. The Ash'ari school rejected any obligation on God but affirmed that God's acts are connected to wisdom and purposes even if those purposes are not rationally constraining. Certain extreme positions in both traditionalist and philosophical circles raised the specter of a divine decree entirely disconnected from any accessible purpose — a pure sovereign will beyond all categories of wisdom or justice.

Ibn al-Qayyim's approach charts a path through these debates that is distinctive in its emphasis on divine wisdom as a positive and knowable quality — not a negation of anthropomorphism but a real attribute of God that provides genuine illumination of why things are as they are.
content_ar: محمد ﷺ ibn Abi Bakr ابن القيم al-Jawziyyah (691–751 AH / 1292–1350 CE) was one of the most prolific and original العلماء in the history of Islamic thought. A student and close companion of ابن تيمية for nearly two decades, he absorbed his teacher's الأثري theological framework and his critique of علم الكلام philosophy بينما developing his own distinctive voice in ethics, spirituality, and the relationship between theological positions and lived religious experience. Shifa al-Alil fi Masa'il al-القضاء wal-القدر wat-Ta'lil wal-Hikma wal-Ta'lil — الشفاء the Sick in Questions of Divine Decree, Predetermination, Causation, and الحكمة — is ابن القيم's comprehensive treatment of divine الحكمة and decree. The title's medical metaphor — الشفاء the sick — suggests the work's practical orientation: the questions it addresses are not merely الأكاديمي but speak to some of the deepest human needs for meaning in the face of المعاناة, الظلم, and divine hiddenness. The الأساسي questions of Shifa al-Alil concern the relationship between divine الحكمة (hikmah), divine will (iradah), and divine decree (القدر). Does God have a purpose in everything He decrees? Do His acts have الحكمة and goodness as their ends, or is God's will an absolute sovereignty without any relationship to purposes beyond the divine self? How should a المؤمن understand المعاناة, الشر, and the apparent injustices of this الدنيا in light of الاعتقاد in an all-knowing, all-powerful, and all-wise God? These questions had been at the center of the great theological controversies of Islamic history. The المعتزلة held that God is obligated by العدل to do what is best for His creatures and cannot decree الظلم. The الأشعري school rejected any الالتزام on God but affirmed that God's acts are connected to الحكمة and purposes even إذا those purposes are not rationally constraining. Certain extreme positions in both traditionalist and philosophical circles raised the specter of a divine decree entirely disconnected from any accessible purpose — a pure sovereign will beyond all categories of الحكمة or العدل. ابن القيم's approach charts a الطريق through these debates that is distinctive in its emphasis on divine الحكمة as a positive and knowable quality — not a negation of anthropomorphism but a real attribute of God that provides genuine illumination of why things are as they are.
